title: Syria's Sharaa names appointees to parliament as US and Iran talk Hormuz
gist: Syria's Sharaa named parliamentary appointees as the US and Iran held talks over the Strait of Hormuz, with Israel seen deriving strategic value from its latest Lebanon deal amid persistent Iran tensions.

insights.claim: Syria's leader Sharaa named appointees to parliament, advancing the country's post-transition political consolidation.
insights.speaker: Al-Monitor
insights.so_what: Institution-building in Syria is a marker for its stability and reconstruction trajectory.

insights.claim: The US and Iran held talks over the Strait of Hormuz.
insights.speaker: Al-Monitor
insights.so_what: Hormuz-focused diplomacy is the key variable for energy-shipping risk.

insights.claim: Israel may see strategic value in its latest deal with Lebanon as tensions with Iran persist.
insights.speaker: Al-Monitor
insights.so_what: An Israel-Lebanon arrangement could reshape the northern front and Iran's leverage.
